levels.name: IRON GATE
levels.desc: A pair of ferrets block the iron gate. One of them snorts. 'Hah — a fox? On the Path of the Sumurai? Go home, kit.'
levels.clear_desc: The ferrets go quiet. One of them laughs despite himself — 'Not bad, fox.' Watching them weave and sidestep, something clicks. Quick Steps. You're going to need that.

levels.name: BAMBOO TRAIL
levels.desc: Boars and rabbits hold the trail. The boars are slow — but the rabbits won't stay still long enough to hit.
levels.defeat_tip: Rabbits are extremely evasive. Don't waste heavy strikes on them — wait for your opening.
levels.clear_desc: The trail is yours. The rabbits were slippery, but you found the gaps. You're starting to understand — speed isn't just about hitting first.

levels.name: FOX DEN
levels.desc: Eight rabbits have made themselves at home in the old fox den. They see you coming — and not one of them runs.
levels.defeat_tip: The rabbits move as a pack — they always strike at the 3rd turn, first action. Let them think they have you. Then make it count.
levels.clear_desc: Eight against one, and not one of them ran. When the last rabbit dropped, you heard slow clapping from the tree line. A turtle steps out of the shadows, arms folded. 'You have skill, fox,' he says. 'Let me show you something.'

levels.name: SILENT SHRINE
levels.desc: The turtle leads you to a weathered shrine at the edge of the forest. Two otters and a ferret wait inside, arms crossed. 'My students,' the turtle says. 'If you can move them, perhaps you are ready to learn what I came here to teach.'
levels.defeat_tip: The turtle is the real threat — Still Wind builds Battle Spirit every moment he's left alone. Break his concentration and it wears off. The otters are consistent: Stream Slash, every turn. Don't count on evading your way through them.
levels.clear_desc: The shrine goes still. The turtle watches the last of his students hit the ground, then exhales slowly. 'Good.' He settles into a low stance — and you see something shift. A faint pulse gathering around him, born not from movement, but from stillness. 'That,' he says, 'is Still Wind. Battle Spirit from the breath, not the blade.' He holds it a moment, then lets it go. 'You will need it before this path is done.'

levels.name: 2 Turtles and a Rabbit
levels.desc: The path ends here — for now. Two turtle masters stand side by side, a rabbit stationed between them. They've been watching you since the shrine. This is their answer.
levels.defeat_tip: This is the hardest fight on the path. Don't rush it. You've seen Still Wind — you know their rhythm. Their patience is their strength, but patience cuts both ways. Find your moment and strike when they breathe.
levels.clear_desc: The last turtle staggers back. The students watching from the ridge go silent. The elder turtle looks at you for a long moment — then bows, just slightly. 'We have not seen a fox fight like that,' he says. The students exchange glances. No one argues.

levels.name: THE FALCON'S EYE
levels.desc: A shape drops from the treetops and lands before you — a falcon, arms folded, eyes sharp. 'I've been watching since the gate,' he says. 'Not bad for a fox. Let's see if you can keep up.'
levels.defeat_tip: The falcon moves fast — faster than anything you've faced. He acts before you can answer. Watch his rhythm and don't give him room to breathe.
levels.clear_desc: The falcon tilts his head — then grins. 'Hah. You actually kept up.' He steps forward and shows you something. One motion, fluid and nearly invisible. 'Shinsoku,' he says. 'Speed your enemy can't answer. Strike before they can think.' He steps aside. 'That's yours now, fox.'

levels.name: FALCON AMBUSH
levels.desc: You don't see them coming. A blur of wings, and a falcon drops from above. Then another. Then three more. They don't look surprised to find you here. They look like they were waiting.
levels.defeat_tip: These falcons are fast — but so are you. Shinsoku exists for this moment. Strike before they can set their footing and the numbers won't matter.
levels.clear_desc: Six falcons on the ground. You stand over all of them, breathing slow. The wind moves through the trees. Somewhere ahead, the Path of the Sumurai continues. You take a step forward.

levels.name: AGAINST THE TIDE
levels.desc: A group of otters blocks the river crossing, laughing and splashing. They see you coming and fall into formation — still grinning. 'A fox on the path?' one calls out. 'Let's see what you've got!'
levels.defeat_tip: Stream Slash rides the current — you can dodge it, but you can't dodge forever. Stop running and fight back. Strike against the tide.
levels.clear_desc: The lead otter splashes over, grinning ear to ear. 'Not bad, fox! Not bad at all!' He shows you the motion — a flowing cut that follows like water. 'Stream Slash,' he says. 'Cuts right through the slippery ones. Doesn't matter how fast they move.' He winks. 'Now you try.'

levels.name: THE GREAT DEBATE
levels.desc: You hear the argument before you see them. A falcon and a turtle are standing in the middle of the path, completely blocking it, deep in what appears to be a very serious disagreement. 'Speed,' the falcon is saying. 'Speed is everything.' The turtle shakes his head slowly. 'Endurance. Outlast everything.' They both stop when they see you. A long pause. Then the falcon says, '...settle this for us?' The turtle cracks his knuckles. 'Yes. Fight us both. Whoever you struggle with more — that one wins.' This is not how you expected this conversation to go.
levels.defeat_tip: The falcon hides behind Quick Steps — Stream Slash cuts right through his dodge, no matter how fast he moves. Stack Shinsoku first to push your own Quick Steps ahead of his, then hit him before he can set it up.
levels.clear_desc: The falcon and the turtle are both on the ground. There is a long silence. The falcon speaks first. 'You were faster than me.' The turtle nods gravely. 'And you outlasted me.' Another silence. They look at each other. Then they look at you. 'So...' the falcon starts. The turtle finishes: '...a fox is better?' You shrug. You don't make the rules. The falcon groans. The turtle puts his face in his hands. Somehow you have made this worse.

levels.name: MEET THE BEAR
levels.desc: A massive bear sumurai blocks the path, arms folded. You study his stance — thick, grounded, immovable. Something about him says he isn't going anywhere. You realize with some discomfort that you two have that in common.
levels.defeat_tip: The bear hits hard and doesn't flinch. Mend your wounds before the second wave — you'll need to be standing when the boars arrive.
levels.clear_desc: The bear goes down on one knee, then looks up. He says nothing for a long moment. Then, slowly, he demonstrates it — a deliberate, focused stillness, and the wound on his arm closes. 'Mend,' he says. 'Because a warrior who cannot endure cannot protect.' He rises. 'Now you know why bears are necessary.'

levels.name: THE TIGER'S EYE
levels.desc: A tiger sumurai steps into the path ahead, lightning crackling at his fingertips. He looks you over — slow, measuring. 'A fox,' he says. Not a question. He smiles. 'Let's see what you've got.'
levels.defeat_tip: The tiger moves with the storm — fast and unpredictable. Survive the first round and read his rhythm. The rabbits in the second wave keep him covered. Don't let them.
levels.clear_desc: The tiger stands there breathing hard, then breaks into a grin. 'Ha — a fox who can keep up with lightning.' He reaches out and taps two fingers against your chest. A current runs through you, quick and sharp. 'Storm Strike,' he says. 'Strike with the storm behind it and they won't see it coming. You've earned it, fox.'

levels.name: THE RABBLE
levels.desc: They come from everywhere at once — rabbits, boars, ferrets, a wall of bodies moving fast. Then you spot it: lightning, flashing from somewhere deep in the crowd. Tigers, mixed in. Waiting.
levels.defeat_tip: Don't get tunnel vision on the small ones — there are tigers buried in the ranks. If you lose track of them, they'll tear you apart while you're busy with the rabble.
levels.clear_desc: The last ferret hits the ground. You stand in the middle of fifteen downed enemies, breathing slow. The path ahead is quiet. You earned that quiet.

levels.name: THE WOLF'S GREETING
levels.desc: A wolf sumurai steps from the treeline, frost curling off his blade. He tilts his head. 'A fox on the Path of the Sumurai?' He looks almost amused. 'Well. Canine blood is canine blood. Let's see if yours runs hot.'
levels.defeat_tip: The wolf is methodical — he doesn't rush. He waits until you overcommit and punishes it. Two wolves in the second wave will try to split your attention. Pick one and finish it fast.
levels.clear_desc: Both wolves hit the ground. The first one sits up slowly, rubbing his jaw — then laughs, low and genuine. 'Ha. Fellow canine, you fight well.' He exchanges a look with the other wolf, who gives a reluctant nod. 'We don't teach this to just anyone,' he says. He draws his blade slow and drags it through the frost — a clean, biting arc. 'Freeze Slash. Cold that lingers. Your enemies will feel it long after the strike.' He sheathes it and steps aside. 'It suits a fox.'
